title: Climbing Stairs
statement: You are climbing a staircase that takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

steps.description: Recognize the recurrence: ways(n) = ways(n-1) + ways(n-2), because the last move is either a 1-step or a 2-step.
Spot that this is the Fibonacci sequence shifted by one.
Identify naive recursion is exponential due to overlapping subproblems.
Decide on bottom-up DP: either an O(n) table or rolling two variables for O(1) space.
Handle the base cases: ways(1) = 1, ways(2) = 2.
State complexity: O(n) time, and O(1) space with the rolling-variable approach (or O(n) with a table).
